# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. bd dolt pull            (pull beads updates from main)
[ ] 4. git commit -m "..."     (commit code changes)
```

**Note:** This is an ephemeral branch (no upstream). Code is merged to main locally, not pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: run `bd dolt pull` at session start
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads updates from Dolt remote
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
bd dolt pull                # Pull latest beads from main
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit your changes
# Merge to main when ready (local merge, not push)
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

## Persistent Memories (4)

Stored via `bd remember`. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`. Remove with `bd forget <key>`.

### issue-102-fully-resolved-with-4-distinct-fixes
Issue #102 fully resolved with 4 distinct fixes (see commits on worktree-102 branch): Bug A=disable stale-tool timer for Claude Code in core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/policy.go; Bug B=new LastWasUserInterrupt field in domain metrics, set only on the '[Request interrupted by user' text marker, used by state_classifier cancellation rule instead of LastToolResultWasError; Bug C=removed toolUseCount/toolResultCount counters in pkg/tailer/tailer.go, HasOpenToolCall now derived from len(lastOpenToolNames) — single source of truth eliminates counter desync on orphan tool_result events from --continue/compact; Bug D=allow-list of terminal stop_reasons {end_turn,stop_sequence,refusal,tool_use} in claudecode/parser.go, everything else (including max_tokens, pause_turn) becomes assistant_streaming. Total flicker reduction across 4 fixtures: 7368 → 1 (the 1 residual is a legitimate 5.8s turn_done-triggered ready episode, not a bug). Replay harness lives at core/cmd/replay-session with scripts in scripts/replay-fixtures.sh and scripts/find-flicker-sessions.sh, fixtures under testdata/issue-102/fixtures.

### issue-102-has-two-distinct-flicker-root-causes
Issue #102 has TWO distinct flicker root causes (replay of current Claude Code session captures both): (1) 15s defaultStaleToolTimeout fires during long Bash runs (working→waiting→working), as previously documented. (2) ClassifyState misinterprets benign tool failures as ESC cancellation: in core/application/services/state_classifier.go the rule '(state==working|waiting) && !HasOpenToolCall && LastEventType==user && LastToolResultWasError → ready (cancellation)' fires whenever any tool returns is_error=true (grep with no matches, failed Bash, etc.), bouncing the session to ready then immediately back to working on the next event. The fix needs to distinguish actual ESC interrupts (user message text starts with '[Request interrupted by user') from normal tool failures. The current claudecode/parser.go at line 84 already detects the ESC text marker — the cancellation rule should check that signal, not just LastToolResultWasError.

### issue-102-root-cause-is-the-15s-defaultstaletooltimeout
Issue #102 root cause is the 15s defaultStaleToolTimeout in core/application/services/session_detector.go, NOT the MessageHistory sliding window. The replay tool (core/cmd/replay-session) shows the pattern clearly: any Bash tool that runs >15s repeatedly trips the stale-tool timer → waiting → tool result arrives → working. On a 558h fixture it produced 82 flickers / 42 stale-timer fires, all on Bash. Fix should bump the timeout for Bash specifically or use a more conservative anchor-event approach. Replay reproductions live in testdata/issue-102/reports/.

### pr-106-bundles-two-flicker-fixes-1-97
PR #106 bundles two flicker fixes: (1) #97 stale-tool timer + StatePolicy abstraction removed (19 files +46/-806); (2) Layer 1 from #102 — Claude Code parser splits [Request interrupted by user] (ESC, sets IsUserInterrupt) from [Request interrupted by user for tool use] (denial, sets new IsToolDenial). Cancellation rule only fires on ESC now, suppressing working→ready→working flicker on every denial. LastWasToolDenial propagates through tailer + domain.SessionMetrics. Layer 2 tracked as GH #108 — uses PermissionRequest hook (NOT Notification), reuses irrlichd HTTP server for ingestion via POST /api/v1/hooks/claudecode, claudecode-adapter-isolated, exposes single generic PermissionPending boolean on shared metrics (avoids reintroducing the deleted StatePolicy abstraction). Key correction: bypassPermissions is NOT a sufficient gate predictor — protected paths (.git/.claude/.vscode/.idea/.husky) still prompt.

title:	Pre-sessions not created when a prior session for the same project exists
state:	OPEN
author:	ingo-eichhorst
labels:	bug
comments:	0
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	113
--
## Symptom

Opening Claude Code in a project no longer produces an immediate `ready` pre-session in the menu bar / popup. The session only appears once the user sends their first message and the `.jsonl` transcript is first written — defeating the pre-session feature (commit a8e06d5).

This only happens in projects where irrlicht already has a session on disk from a previous daemon run (which is the common case given the default `MaxSessionAge` of 5 days).

## Root cause

`core/cmd/irrlichd/main.go:282-297` wires a `sessionChecker` callback into the process `Scanner` that returns `true` whenever **any** non-proc session with a matching `projectDir` exists in the repo:

```go
procScanner.WithSessionChecker(func(projectDir string) bool {
    sessions, _ := cachedRepo.ListAll()
    for _, s := range sessions {
        if strings.HasPrefix(s.SessionID, \"proc-\") { continue }
        if s.TranscriptPath != \"\" &&
            filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(s.TranscriptPath)) == projectDir {
            return true
        }
    }
    return false
})
```

`core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/scanner.go:205` calls `hasActiveSession(projectDir)` in `poll()` before emitting a `proc-<pid>` pre-session. `hasActiveSession` consults the `sessionChecker` first and only falls back to a 60s file-mtime check. Because the callback matches on `projectDir` alone, **historical sessions from prior runs** suppress pre-session creation for every new Claude Code process in a previously-used project.

The callback was introduced in commit 0243172 to prevent duplicate pre-sessions after a daemon restart while Claude Code is still running (real session's transcript mtime is >60s but the process is alive). It overcorrected.

## Proposed fix

Tighten the callback to discriminate by PID:

1. Change `sessionChecker` signature to `func(projectDir string, pid int) bool` in `scanner.go:45,78`.
2. Update `hasActiveSession` (`scanner.go:259`) to take a PID and pass it through.
3. Update both `poll()` call sites (`scanner.go:185,205`) to pass the current PID.
4. In `main.go:282-297`, add `s.PID == pid` to the match condition.

Correctness across scenarios:
- **Daemon restart, Claude Code still running**: `seedFromDisk` → `pidMgr.SeedPIDs` re-attaches live PIDs to persisted sessions. Callback returns `true`, no duplicate pre-session. ✓
- **Historical session from yesterday, new process today**: yesterday's session has a stale/zero PID (dead-PID cleanup in `SeedPIDs`). Callback returns `false`, pre-session is created. ✓
- **Pre-session → real-session supersession**: the 60s file-mtime fallback in `hasActiveSession` still catches freshly written transcripts, so scanner still marks the tracked pre-session as superseded. ✓

## Files

- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/scanner.go` (lines 45, 78, 185, 205, 259-288)
- `core/cmd/irrlichd/main.go` (lines 282-297)
- `core/e2e/presession_test.go` — add regression test: pre-seed `memRepo` with a session whose `PID != fake process PID` and `TranscriptPath` under the project dir; assert `proc-<pid>` pre-session appears within 5s.

## Verification

1. `cd core && go test ./adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/... ./application/services/... ./e2e/...`
2. **Manual**: ensure a session file for the current project already exists in `~/.irrlicht/sessions/`. Rebuild + restart daemon. Open Claude Code in the same project. **Expected**: `ready` session appears immediately in the menu bar / popup, before any user input.
3. **Daemon-restart regression**: open Claude Code, send a message, restart `irrlichd` while Claude Code stays open. Expect exactly one session (the real one, with PID tracked) — no extra `proc-<pid>` pre-session alongside it.

## Refs

- Pre-session feature intro: a8e06d5
- `sessionChecker` intro (the overcorrection): 0243172
- Refactor to `processlifecycle` (preserved the bug): 931ec88

The callback was introduced in commit 0243172 to prevent duplicate pre-sessions after a daemon restart while Claude Code is still running (real session's transcript mtime is >60s but the process is alive). It overcorrected.

## Proposed fix

Tighten the callback to discriminate by PID:

1. Change `sessionChecker` signature to `func(projectDir string, pid int) bool` in `scanner.go:45,78`.
2. Update `hasActiveSession` (`scanner.go:259`) to take a PID and pass it through.
3. Update both `poll()` call sites (`scanner.go:185,205`) to pass the current PID.
4. In `main.go:282-297`, add `s.PID == pid` to the match condition.

Correctness across scenarios:
- **Daemon restart, Claude Code still running**: `seedFromDisk` → `pidMgr.SeedPIDs` re-attaches live PIDs to persisted sessions. Callback returns `true`, no duplicate pre-session. ✓
- **Historical session from yesterday, new process today**: yesterday's session has a stale/zero PID (dead-PID cleanup in `SeedPIDs`). Callback returns `false`, pre-session is created. ✓
- **Pre-session → real-session supersession**: the 60s file-mtime fallback in `hasActiveSession` still catches freshly written transcripts, so scanner still marks the tracked pre-session as superseded. ✓

## Files

- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/scanner.go` (lines 45, 78, 185, 205, 259-288)
- `core/cmd/irrlichd/main.go` (lines 282-297)
- `core/e2e/presession_test.go` — add regression test: pre-seed `memRepo` with a session whose `PID != fake process PID` and `TranscriptPath` under the project dir; assert `proc-<pid>` pre-session appears within 5s.

## Verification

1. `cd core && go test ./adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/... ./application/services/... ./e2e/...`
2. **Manual**: ensure a session file for the current project already exists in `~/.irrlicht/sessions/`. Rebuild + restart daemon. Open Claude Code in the same project. **Expected**: `ready` session appears immediately in the menu bar / popup, before any user input.
3. **Daemon-restart regression**: open Claude Code, send a message, restart `irrlichd` while Claude Code stays open. Expect exactly one session (the real one, with PID tracked) — no extra `proc-<pid>` pre-session alongside it.

## Refs

- Pre-session feature intro: a8e06d5
- `sessionChecker` intro (the overcorrection): 0243172
- Refactor to `processlifecycle` (preserved the bug): 931ec88

45		}

52		scanner := processlifecycle.NewScanner(processName, "test", projectsRoot, 200*time.Millisecond)
53		repo := &memRepo{states: make(map[string]*session.SessionState)}
54

56			[]inbound.AgentWatcher{scanner},

# Fix: Pre-sessions not created when prior session exists for project (gh #113)

## Context

Pre-sessions (the `proc-<pid>` placeholder that lets a `ready` Claude Code
session show up in the menu bar before the user sends their first message,
introduced in commit a8e06d5) silently stop working for any project that has
ever been opened before. Because `MaxSessionAge` defaults to 5 days, this is
the common case — effectively disabling the pre-session feature for daily
users.

Root cause: the `sessionChecker` callback wired into the process scanner in
`core/cmd/irrlichd/main.go:282-297` treats the project directory as the
identity of a session. Any historical session on disk for the same project
makes the check return `true`, which causes
`processlifecycle/scanner.go:205` to skip emitting a new `proc-<pid>` event
for a freshly-opened Claude Code process.

The callback was introduced in commit 0243172 to prevent **duplicate**
pre-sessions after a daemon restart while a real Claude Code session was
still running (the real session's transcript mtime could exceed 60s, so the
file-mtime fallback alone wasn't enough). That correction was valid, but the
match condition was too broad.

The fix is to make `sessionChecker` discriminate by **PID** in addition to
`projectDir`: a persisted real session suppresses a new `proc-<pid>` only
when it belongs to the *same live process*.

## Approach

Thread the polling PID through `Scanner.hasActiveSession` and into the
injected `sessionChecker`, and extend the callback in `main.go` to require
`s.PID == pid` for a match.

### Files to modify

1. `core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/scanner.go`
   - Line 45: change the field type to
     `sessionChecker func(projectDir string, pid int) bool`.
   - Lines 74–81: update `WithSessionChecker` signature and doc comment to
     take `func(projectDir string, pid int) bool`.
   - Line 259: change `hasActiveSession` signature to
     `hasActiveSession(projectDir string, pid int) bool` and pass `pid` into
     the `sessionChecker` call at line 263. The 60s mtime fallback (lines
     266–286) does not change — it is still project-scoped and still
     correctly catches freshly-written transcripts that haven't yet been
     persisted as sessions in the repo.
   - Lines 185 and 205: pass the current `pid` loop variable into
     `s.hasActiveSession(projectDir, pid)`.

2. `core/cmd/irrlichd/main.go`
   - Lines 282–297: update the callback to
     `func(projectDir string, pid int) bool` and add `s.PID == pid` to the
     match condition alongside the existing `TranscriptPath` / `projectDir`
     check. No new imports needed; `strings` and `filepath` are already in
     scope.

3. `core/e2e/presession_test.go` — new regression test
   `TestPreSession_CreatedDespiteHistoricalSession`:
   - Start a fake process the same way as
     `TestPreSession_DetectedBeforeTranscript`
     (`presession_test.go:27-102`).
   - Before starting the scanner, pre-seed `memRepo` with a historical
     session whose `PID` is something *other than* the fake process PID
     (e.g. `99999`) and whose `TranscriptPath` is
     `<projectsRoot>/<encoded-cwd>/old.jsonl`, with

# Build & Restart macOS Dev Stack

Build the irrlicht daemon and Swift app, then replace all running instances with the new builds.

## Steps

1. **Build the Go daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && go build -o bin/irrlichd ./cmd/irrlichd
   ```

2. **Build the Swift app**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos && swift build 2>&1 | tail -5
   ```

3. **Kill all running irrlicht processes** (production app, production daemon, debug app, debug daemon)
   ```bash
   pkill -f "Irrlicht.app" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f irrlichd 2>/dev/null
   sleep 2
   ```

4. **Clean up stale socket**
   ```bash
   rm -f /Users/ingo/.local/share/irrlicht/irrlichd.sock
   ```

content.text:
Dev stack up: daemon PID 18659 (from worktree build, fix included), Swift app PID 18901. Daemon is serving the current Claude Code session correctly.

## How to manually test the fix

The bug: opening Claude Code in a project that already has a session on disk skipped the `proc-<pid>` pre-session, so no `ready` session would appear in the menu bar until you sent the first message.

**Reproduction steps:**

1. **Confirm a prior session exists for some project** (pick one you've used recently that's in `~/.irrlicht/sessions/`):
   ```
   ls ~/.local/share/irrlicht/sessions/ 2>/dev/null || ls ~/.irrlicht/sessions/ 2>/dev/null
   ```
   Pick any `<uuid>.json` file and note its `project_name` / `cwd`.

2. **Open Claude Code in that same project directory** (new terminal: `cd <that project>` then `claude`).

3. **Observe the menu bar immediately** — before you type anything.
   - **Expected (fix working):** A `ready` session for that project shows up within ~1 second, showing the new `proc-<pid>` entry. Confirm it's the new one by querying the API:
     ```
     curl -s http://localhost:7837/api/v1/sessions | python3 -m json.tool | grep -E 'proc-|session_id|cwd' | head
     ```
     You should see a `proc-<pid>` session alongside any real ones.
   - **Bug behaviour (before fix):** No new entry shows up until you send your first message.

4. **Regression check — daemon restart with a live Claude Code session** (this is the scenario the original overcorrection was guarding; make sure it still works):
   - Open Claude Code, send a message so the real session is persisted (non-empty transcript, real PID).
   - `pkill -f "bin/irrlichd"` then restart the daemon as above.
   - **Expected:** exactly one session for that project (the real one, with its PID). No extra `proc-<pid>` alongside it.

5. **Quick E2E sanity** (no manual UI work): the regression test encodes step 3 — if you want to rerun it:
   ```
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/.claude/worktrees/113/core && go test ./e2e/ -run TestPreSession_CreatedDespiteHistoricalSession -v -count=1
   ```

Logs if anything looks off: `/tmp/irrlichd-dev.log`, `/tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log`.

Note: I built the daemon from the **worktree** (`/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/.claude/worktrees/113/core`), not the main repo path the skill hardcodes, because the fix only lives on the `worktree-113` branch — building from the main repo path would have tested the old broken code.

content.text:
Good news — **the fix is working**. `proc-21130` is now in the daemon API:

```
proc-21130 | ready | /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht | pid=21130
```

And the daemon log confirms it at `23:16:07`:
```
proc-21130 | new session detected in -Users-ingo-projects-irrlicht (adapter=claude-code)
proc-21130 | discovered pid 21130 for claude-code session
```

This is exactly what the bug prevented before: a new Claude Code process opening in `/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht` where another session (`45af9457`, pid 80814) already exists. Without the fix the scanner's `sessionChecker` would have returned true for the project dir and no pre-session would have been emitted. With the fix, `s.PID != pid` causes it to correctly skip the existing session and create `proc-21130`.

No transcript for `28db68ae-c36a-42e0-986f-83ce6e8b17e5` exists on disk yet — that file only gets written when you send your first message, at which point `proc-21130` will be cleaned up and replaced by the real session.

**If the macOS menu bar isn't showing it**, that's a separate UI / refresh path, not the daemon. A few things to check:
1. The Swift app connects to the daemon on startup — if it was launched before the daemon was listening on 7837, it may not be getting updates. Click the menu bar icon to see current state, or kill and re-launch:
   ```
   pkill -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" && nohup /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/.claude/worktrees/113/platforms/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/Irrlicht > /tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```
2. Check the Swift app log: `tail /tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log`
3. The project group in the menu bar may be collapsed — `proc-21130` should appear under the `irrlicht` project alongside `45af9457`.

Want me to restart the Swift app, or should I look at the app log to see what it's receiving?

content.text:
Found the root cause. Let me check the timeline from the logs:

```
23:16:07.513  proc-21130  new session detected
23:16:07.800  proc-21130  discovered pid 21130
23:16:38.396  proc-21130  session removed    ← deleted after ~30s
```

No "real session arrived" log before the removal, so it wasn't the normal supersession path. Let me trace what deleted it.

The scanner's supersession check in `scanner.go:185-202` uses `hasActiveSession(projectDir, pid)`. That function consults the PID-aware `sessionChecker` first — but when that returns false, it **falls back to a project-wide jsonl mtime check** (`scanner.go:275-286`):

```go
// Checks ANY .jsonl in the project dir modified within 60s
for _, e := range entries {
    if info.ModTime().After(cutoff) {
        return true
    }
}
```

This is the bug hiding underneath the GH #113 fix. What happened to your session:

1. `proc-21130` was created correctly (mtime of `45af9457.jsonl` was stale at that moment — no activity on the main-repo session for a minute).
2. Between `23:16:07` and `23:16:38` the other active session (`45af9457`, PID 80814) in `/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht` wrote to its jsonl.
3. On the next scanner poll, `alreadyTracked` fired for PID 21130 → called `hasActiveSession` → `sessionChecker` correctly returned `false` (no non-proc session has PID 21130) → **but the mtime fallback saw `45af9457.jsonl` with fresh mtime and returned `true`**.
4. Scanner marked `proc-21130` as superseded, emitted `EventRemoved`.
5. `onRemoved` (`session_detector.go:489`) saw `TranscriptPath == ""` → deleted the pre-session entirely.

So GH #113's PID-aware `sessionChecker` works — but the project-wide mtime fallback undoes it whenever a *different* session in the same project has recent transcript writes.

**Fix**: the mtime fallback in `hasActiveSession` is fundamentally wrong — it can't tell which jsonl belongs to which PID. The PID-aware `sessionChecker` is the correct mechanism and already covers all the cases the fallback was trying to catch (idle real sessions, daemon-restart seeding). I'll remove the mtime fallback.

Fix verified in production. The logs tell the whole story:

```
# Before fix (first daemon run):
23:16:07.513  proc-21130  new session detected
23:16:07.800  proc-21130  discovered pid 21130
23:16:38.396  proc-21130  session removed           ← bug: mtime fallback superseded it

# After fix (second daemon run):
23:32:23.364  proc-21130  new session detected
23:32:23.654  proc-21130  discovered pid 21130
                          (no removal — stable)
```

## Summary of the second fix

**File changed**: `core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/scanner.go`

Removed the project-wide `.jsonl` mtime fallback from `hasActiveSession()`. The old fallback returned `true` whenever *any* transcript in the project dir had been modified within 60 seconds, which wrongly suppressed pre-sessions for new processes whenever a neighbouring session in the same project was actively being written to. `scanner.hasActiveSession` now trusts the PID-aware `sessionChecker` as the sole signal — which is correct because the checker already handles all the cases the fallback was trying to catch (idle real sessions, daemon-restart seeding).

**Test added**: `TestPreSession_SurvivesNeighbourSessionActivity` in `core/e2e/presession_test.go`. It creates a fake claude process, seeds a neighbour `.jsonl` with a continuously refreshed mtime, and asserts the pre-session appears AND stays alive across ~7 scanner polls. Test-the-test: confirmed it fails with the mtime fallback reintroduced (fails at initial detection, which actually revealed the mtime fallback was wrong in both the initial-detection path AND the supersession path — removing it fixes both).

**Current modified files** (still uncommitted, per your workflow):
```
core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/scanner.go
core/cmd/irrlichd/main.go
core/e2e/presession_test.go
```

Dev daemon is running at PID 53873 on port 7837. New claude processes in projects with active neighbour sessions now get their `proc-<pid>` pre-session correctly. Let me know when you're ready to commit, or if you want to test more scenarios first.

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(pre-session): discriminate by PID so neighbours don't suppress new sessions (#113)

Two overlapping bugs kept proc-<pid> pre-sessions from appearing whenever
a new Claude Code process opened in a project that already had a session
on disk or a neighbour with recent transcript activity — hiding every new
session from the menu bar until the user sent a first message.

1. The sessionChecker in main.go matched by projectDir alone, so any
   historical session within the 5-day MaxSessionAge window blocked
   pre-session creation for every new claude process in that project.
   Thread the polling PID through scanner.hasActiveSession and into
   sessionChecker, and require s.PID == pid in the match condition.

2. scanner.hasActiveSession also fell back to a project-wide .jsonl mtime
   check that returned true whenever ANY transcript in the dir had been
   modified within 60s — including transcripts owned by other live
   sessions. This both suppressed initial pre-session creation when a
   neighbour was active AND marked freshly-created pre-sessions as
   superseded on the next poll, making onRemoved delete them entirely.
   The mtime check cannot discriminate which jsonl belongs to which PID,
   and the now-PID-aware sessionChecker already covers every scenario it
   was trying to catch (idle real sessions, daemon-restart seeding).
   Remove it.

Adds two regression tests in core/e2e/presession_test.go:
- TestPreSession_CreatedDespiteHistoricalSession: a historical session
  with a different live PID must not suppress the new pre-session.
- TestPreSession_SurvivesNeighbourSessionActivity: a neighbour jsonl with
  fresh mtime must not cause scanner to mark the pre-session superseded.

Verified live: opening a new Claude Code in /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht
while another active session wrote to its transcript now produces a
stable proc-<pid> immediately, both via the HTTP API and irrlicht-ls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
